breakClass: .ToList()/.ToArray() (or a nested correlated-collection projection) embedded inside a server-side EF Core 10 query â€” either in a `let` clause whose value is consumed by a server `select new {...}`/`where ... .Contains(...)`, or as an inline `Field = (from ... let X=db....ToList() ... select ...).ToList()` sub-collection â€” on an outer IQueryable that has NOT yet crossed an .AsEnumerable()/.ToList() materialization boundary. EF6 client-evaluated these; EF Core 10 throws "could not be translated" (500). Outer queries that are materialized first (var data2 = v.ToList(); from o in data2 ...) or sourced from db.X.AsEnumerable() are SAFE.
why: db.Reminders source with no materialization boundary; let remas = db.ReminderAssigneds....ToList() is consumed by where remas.Contains(empId), forcing server-side translation of the embedded .ToList(); additionally the AssignedTo correlated collection .ToList() is in the same server projection (enumerated at line 334). EF Core 10 cannot translate the .ToList() in the predicate. Port regression.
finalFix:
In QuickNetCore/Controllers/HomeController.cs (GetReminder), remove the `let remas = ...ToList()` line (309) and rewrite the membership test as a correlated EXISTS that EF Core 10 translates natively. Replace lines 309-311:

    let remas = db.ReminderAssigneds.Where(x => x.ReminderId == a.ReminderId).Select(x => x.EmployeeId).ToList()
    where olddate <= a.RDate && a.RDate <= newdate &&
    remas.Contains(empId)

with:

    where olddate <= a.RDate && a.RDate <= newdate &&
    db.ReminderAssigneds.Any(x => x.ReminderId == a.ReminderId && x.EmployeeId == empId)

`db.ReminderAssigneds.Any(x => x.ReminderId == a.ReminderId && x.EmployeeId == empId)` is logically identical to `remas.Contains(empId)` (both = "an assignment exists for this reminder whose EmployeeId == empId") and translates to SQL EXISTS(...). Leave the AssignedTo correlated-collection .ToList() projection unchanged â€” EF Core 10 translates it fine. Output is byte-for-byte identical to legacy EF6. (If an environment ever still chokes, the equivalent fallback is to add `.AsEnumerable()` immediately after the from/join/where clauses to force client evaluation, mirroring EF6 â€” but the .Any() rewrite is preferred and keeps the filter server-side.)
reasoning:
I independently inspected QuickNetCore/Controllers/HomeController.cs lines 290-336 (method GetReminder, [HttpPost] JSON grid) and confirmed the flag.

(a) SERVER-SIDE: The outer query `var v = (from a in db.Reminders join b in db.Users ... let remas = ... where ... remas.Contains(empId) ... select new {...})` is built from db.Reminders (IQueryable) with NO .ToList()/.ToArray()/.AsEnumerable() boundary before it is enumerated. `v` is reassigned (line 333 OrderByDescending/Take, still IQueryable) and only materialized at `var data = v.ToList()` (line 334). So the `let remas = db.ReminderAssigneds.Where(x => x.ReminderId == a.ReminderId).Select(x => x.EmployeeId).ToList()` (line 309) and its consumer `where ... remas.Contains(empId)` (line 311) are inside the expression tree EF Core 10 must translate to SQL. Both db.Reminders and db.ReminderAssigneds are real DbSets (ApplicationDbContext.cs lines 360/362); empId is a pre-materialized scalar long (line 304, FirstOrDefault before the query); ReminderAssigned.EmployeeId/ReminderId are long columns. This is NOT a group-aggregate over an IGrouping and NOT a standalone top-level query.

(b) WOULD 500: The inline `.ToList()` on a correlated subquery embedded inside a still-server-side predicate has no SQL translation. EF Core 10 fails to translate `remas.Contains(empId)` (where `remas` is an in-query Enumerable.ToList of a correlated subquery) and throws InvalidOperationException "could not be translated" at query-compilation time -> unhandled -> HTTP 500. Because the failure is at SQL generation (before execution), it 500s on EVERY call regardless of data (even empty tables) -> severity HIGH. This is exactly the documented port-break class.

PORT REGRESSION confirmed: legacy EF6 at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/HomeController.cs lines 338-365 is byte-identical (same `let remas = ...ToList()` + `remas.Contains(empId)`). EF6 client-evaluated the LINQ-to-Objects Contains over the materialized list; EF Core 10 does not. Pure regression.

The AssignedTo correlated-collection projection `(from aa in db.ReminderAssigneds ... select new {bb.FirstName, bb.LastName}).ToList()` inside `select new {...}` is NOT the break â€” EF Core 10 natively translates correlated collection projections (split/JSON subquery), so it can stay as-is. The break is solely the `let remas ... .ToList()` predicate.

breakClass: DateTime arithmetic helper inside an unmaterialized query: DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays/AddMinutes (plain CLR helpers in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs, NOT registered EF functions) applied to a COLUMN expression inside a server-side LINQ-to-Entities projection or Where. EF6 used DbFunctions.AddDays (canonical -> SQL DATEADD); the port swapped in DbFunctionsCompat.* which EF Core 10 cannot translate, so the query throws 'could not be translated' -> HTTP 500. Fix = EF-native (col).Value.AddDays(n ?? 0) which DOES translate to DATEADD.
why: select new of IQueryable v, then v.OrderByDescending().Take(5) (L231) then v.ToList() (L232) materializes server-side; no AsEnumerable boundary. CLR helper untranslatable -> 500. Dashboard quotation widget. Legacy DbFunctions.AddDays. Port regression.
finalFix:
Replace line 229 (Controllers/HomeController.cs, method GetQuotEntry) so the date math uses EF-native DateTime.AddDays (translates to SQL DATEADD) instead of the untranslatable DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays. Note QuotDate is non-nullable DateTime so NO `.Value` (the hunter's `b.QuotDate.Value` would not compile):

validity = (b.QuotDate.AddDays((b.QuotValidity == null) ? 0 : (b.QuotValidity.Value + 1)) >= DateTime.Now) ? "Active" : "Expired"

This mirrors legacy EF6 exactly: legacy computed `DateTime.Now <= QuotDate + days ? "Active" : "Expired"`; here `QuotDate.AddDays(days) >= DateTime.Now` is the same predicate. The days argument is a non-nullable int (0 when QuotValidity is null, else QuotValidity.Value + 1), matching the legacy semantics where days is never null in practice. The identical pattern also exists at Models/Common.cs:9931 (Quotation, same fix) and Models/Common.cs:10817 (PurchaseQuotation: `b.PQuotDate.AddDays((b.PQuotValidity == null) ? 0 : (b.PQuotValidity.Value + 1)) >= DateTime.Now` â€” PQuotDate is also non-nullable DateTime, PQuotValidity is int?); both should get the same treatment if those endpoints are live.
reasoning:
I independently confirmed the hunter's claim at Controllers/HomeController.cs:229 in method GetQuotEntry.

(a) SERVER-SIDE â€” CONFIRMED. The projection `select new {... validity = (DateTime.Now <= DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(b.QuotDate, (b.QuotValidity == null) ? 0 : b.QuotValidity + 1)) ? "Active" : "Expired" }` is composed into IQueryable `v` (lines 220-230). Line 231 `v = v.OrderByDescending(c => c.QuotationId).Take(5)` keeps it IQueryable. Line 232 `var data = v.ToList()` is the FIRST and ONLY enumeration â€” no `.AsEnumerable()`/`.ToList()`/`.AsAsyncEnumerable()` boundary precedes it, and this is not a standalone top-level query nor a group-aggregate over an IGrouping. So the AddDays call is translated into the SQL projection.

(b) WOULD 500 â€” CONFIRMED, HIGH severity. `DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays` is a plain static CLR helper in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs:70-71 (`public static DateTime? AddDays(DateTime? date, int? days) => date?.AddDays(days ?? 0);`). It is NOT registered with EF Core via HasDbFunction, so EF Core 10 has no SQL translation for it. Inside a server-side projection EF Core 10 throws InvalidOperationException 'The LINQ expression ... could not be translated' at ToList() time -> HTTP 500. This breaks the entire query for ANY returned row (the projection itself cannot be built), so it 500s on realistic data, not just edge data.

PORT REGRESSION â€” CONFIRMED. Legacy EF6 at _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/HomeController.cs:261 used `DbFunctions.AddDays(b.QuotDate, ...)`, a canonical function EF6 translated to SQL DATEADD. The port mechanically swapped in the untranslatable `DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays`.

ENTITY TYPES verified in Models/Quotation.cs: QuotDate is non-nullable DateTime (line 13); QuotValidity is int? (line 43). This matters for the fix: the hunter's proposed `b.QuotDate.Value` would NOT COMPILE because QuotDate is a plain DateTime, not Nullable<DateTime>. The correct EF-native form omits `.Value`. `DateTime.AddDays` IS translatable by EF Core 10 to DATEADD, preserving the original DATEADD behavior.
